gloss.en: planned for a particular time

words.example: The flight is scheduled to leave at noon.

gloss.en: a plan or system for doing something

words.example: They started a new training scheme.

gloss.en: a person who has studied a subject deeply

words.example: She is a respected classical scholar.

gloss.en: money given to help pay for education

words.example: She won a scholarship to study at Stanford.

gloss.en: a medieval system of religious philosophy

words.example: He wrote a book about medieval scholasticism.

gloss.en: a girl who goes to school

words.example: The schoolgirl wore a blue uniform.

gloss.en: in a way relating to science

words.example: The claim has not been scientifically proven.

gloss.en: a light two-wheeled vehicle or toy

words.example: He rode his scooter to work.

gloss.en: to move or climb quickly using your hands

words.example: They had to scramble up the steep hill.

gloss.en: a thin mark or cut on a surface

words.example: There is a scratch on the car door.

gloss.en: a loud, high cry of fear or pain

words.example: She let out a scream of pain.

gloss.en: the flat surface where pictures or text appear

words.example: He stared at the computer screen.

gloss.en: the showing of a film or programme

words.example: This is the movie's first screening here.

gloss.en: careless or messy writing or drawing

words.example: I could not read his scribble.

gloss.en: a person who hates spending money

words.example: Don't be such a scrooge at Christmas.

gloss.en: equipment for breathing underwater while diving

words.example: They learned to dive with scuba gear.

gloss.en: a tool with a long curved blade for cutting grass

words.example: The farmer cut the grass with a scythe.

gloss.en: happening or changing with the seasons

words.example: Fruit pickers do seasonal work.

gloss.en: a place where you can sit

words.example: She sat back in her seat.

gloss.en: connected with teaching children aged 11 to 18

words.example: He teaches at a secondary school.

gloss.en: used to introduce a second point

words.example: Firstly it's costly, and secondly it's slow.

gloss.en: a part of a country's economy or activity

words.example: She works in the banking sector.

gloss.en: safe and likely to last a long time

words.example: He finally found a secure job.

gloss.en: to look for something or someone

words.example: Drivers should seek alternative routes.

gloss.en: a person trying to find or get something

words.example: He is a constant attention seeker.

gloss.en: a long board children sit on and rock

words.example: The children played on the seesaw.

gloss.en: to choose someone or something from a group

words.example: He was not selected for the team.

gloss.en: the process of choosing from a group

words.example: She took a long time to make her selection.

gloss.en: the type of person you usually are

words.example: You'll soon be your old self again.

gloss.en: belief in your own abilities

words.example: The job gave her real self-confidence.

gloss.en: sure about your own abilities

words.example: She is a calm, self-confident speaker.

gloss.en: caring more about others than yourself

words.example: Her selfless work helped many people.

gloss.en: a person who sells something

words.example: The seller agreed to lower the price.

gloss.en: a match just before the final

words.example: Our team reached the semi-final.

gloss.en: the punctuation mark ( ; )

words.example: Use a semicolon to join the clauses.

gloss.en: a small class for discussing a topic

words.example: Teaching is by lectures and seminars.

gloss.en: high in rank or status

words.example: She is a senior manager here.

gloss.en: an ability to understand or judge something

words.example: He has a great sense of humour.

gloss.en: able to make good, practical decisions

words.example: She's a sensible sort of person.

gloss.en: aware of others' feelings

words.example: He is a sensitive and caring man.

gloss.en: a group of words expressing a complete idea

words.example: Does the sentence contain an adverb?

gloss.en: apart from others; not together

words.example: We paid for our meals separately.

gloss.en: a set of things in a particular order

words.example: He described the sequence of events.

gloss.en: calm, peaceful and untroubled

words.example: The lake looked calm and serene.

gloss.en: a person who works in another's house

words.example: They employed a domestic servant.

gloss.en: a period of time doing an activity

words.example: We had a long training session.

gloss.en: a problem that delays progress

words.example: The project suffered a major setback.

gloss.en: to end an argument or disagreement

words.example: They met to settle the dispute.

gloss.en: a person who goes to live in a new region

words.example: Most of the settlers came from England.

gloss.en: extremely bad or serious

words.example: His injuries are severe.

gloss.en: very badly or seriously

words.example: He was severely injured in the crash.

gloss.en: unfair treatment based on a person's sex

words.example: They are fighting sexism at work.

gloss.en: connected with sex

words.example: The film explores sexual behaviour.

gloss.en: sexually attractive

words.example: She fancies the sexy lead singer.

gloss.en: old, worn and in poor condition

words.example: He wore a shabby old coat.

gloss.en: a cool, dark area out of the sun

words.example: We sat in the shade of a tree.

gloss.en: a dark shape made when light is blocked

words.example: The children chased each other's shadows.

gloss.en: upset or shocked by something

words.example: She was badly shaken by the news.

gloss.en: relating to Shakespeare or his works

words.example: The actor loves shakespearean drama.

gloss.en: shaking, weak or not steady

words.example: Her voice was shaky with fear.

gloss.en: not deep

words.example: The water here is very shallow.

gloss.en: something sad or disappointing

words.example: It's a great shame she is leaving.

gloss.en: the outer form of something

words.example: The cake has a round shape.

gloss.en: having a particular shape

words.example: The balloon was shaped like a cow.

gloss.en: suddenly and by a large amount

words.example: Prices rose sharply last month.

gloss.en: a place to live or stay safely

words.example: People need food, clothing and shelter.

gloss.en: something used to protect from danger

words.example: The soldier raised his shield.

gloss.en: a period of work by a group of workers

words.example: She works the night shift.

gloss.en: a large boat that travels on the sea

words.example: The ship carried goods across the sea.

gloss.en: the destruction of a ship at sea

words.example: Few sailors survived the shipwreck.

gloss.en: a strong feeling of surprise, usually bad

words.example: I got a terrible shock the other day.

gloss.en: surprised and upset

words.example: I was quite shocked by the news.

gloss.en: very offensive or upsetting

words.example: Everyone talked about his shocking behaviour.

gloss.en: an event where someone is shot with a gun

words.example: Police are investigating the shooting.

gloss.en: a person who owns or runs a shop

words.example: The shopkeeper opened the store early.

gloss.en: to steal goods from a shop

words.example: He was caught trying to shoplift.

gloss.en: a person who steals from shops

words.example: The guard stopped a shoplifter.

gloss.en: the crime of stealing from shops

words.example: Shoplifting costs stores a lot of money.

gloss.en: the land along the edge of the sea or a lake

words.example: We walked along the sandy shore.

gloss.en: lasting only a short time

words.example: They took out a short-term loan.

gloss.en: a situation when there is not enough

words.example: The city faces a water shortage.

gloss.en: soon; in a short time

words.example: She arrived shortly after us.

gloss.en: the state of being short or brief

words.example: He complained of shortness of breath.

gloss.en: the act or sound of firing a gun

words.example: We heard some shots in the distance.

gloss.en: the entertainment business

words.example: She has worked in showbiz for years.

gloss.en: an event that shows the best of something

words.example: The fair is a showcase for new talent.

gloss.en: a brother or sister

words.example: He often argues with his sibling.

gloss.en: your name written in your own way

words.example: She forged his signature on the cheque.

gloss.en: the importance of something

words.example: It was a decision of major significance.

gloss.en: large or important enough to be noticed

words.example: There are no significant differences here.

gloss.en: in an important or noticeable way

words.example: Sales were significantly higher this year.

gloss.en: to be a sign of; to mean

words.example: A red light can signify danger.

gloss.en: a complete lack of noise

words.example: Their footsteps echoed in the silence.

gloss.en: fine soft thread made by silkworms

words.example: She wore a smooth silk scarf.

gloss.en: objects made of or covered with silver

words.example: They polished the family silverware.

gloss.en: the act of making something simpler

words.example: The new rules are a useful simplification.

gloss.en: happening at the same time

words.example: There was a simultaneous translation.

gloss.en: from a past time until now

words.example: I have not seen her since Monday.

gloss.en: honest and showing true feelings

words.example: Please accept my sincere apology.

gloss.en: the quality of being honest and genuine

words.example: I doubt the sincerity of his promise.

gloss.en: the sister of your husband or wife

words.example: My sister-in-law lives next door.

gloss.en: a short board on wheels you ride

words.example: He fell off his skateboard.

gloss.en: a person who rides a skateboard

words.example: The skateboarder did a clever trick.

gloss.en: good at doing something

words.example: She is a skilful painter.

gloss.en: in a skilful way

words.example: He skilfully repaired the old clock.

gloss.en: having the ability to do something well

words.example: They hired a skilled engineer.

gloss.en: good at doing something

words.example: He is a skillful driver.

gloss.en: in a skilful way

words.example: She skillfully answered every question.

gloss.en: to read quickly to get the main points

words.example: I only had time to skim the report.

gloss.en: the bones of the head

words.example: He suffered a fractured skull.

gloss.en: a small bird that sings while flying high

words.example: A skylark sang high above the field.

gloss.en: toward the sky

words.example: The rocket shot skyward at dawn.

gloss.en: toward the sky

words.example: She gazed skywards at the stars.

gloss.en: very informal words used by a group

words.example: Teenagers use a lot of slang.

gloss.en: the killing of many people or animals

words.example: The war led to terrible slaughter.

gloss.en: a person owned and forced to work

words.example: He was once a slave on a farm.

gloss.en: one page of an electronic presentation

words.example: Here's a slide showing target markets.

gloss.en: very small in degree

words.example: There was a slight change in the plan.

gloss.en: to slide by accident and nearly fall

words.example: She slipped and fell on the ice.

words.example: He cut a thin sliver of cheese.
